title: Test coverage for secretscan - unit tests over 9 patterns + walker semantics

evidence.ref: Audit found 6 internal/ packages without test files. secretscan is security-critical (gates all PRs via security.yml) but had no unit tests — any pattern regression would only be caught by a real secret slipping into a test fixture, which itself would be a security incident
036-bcs-enum - established the test-file convention for the decision package; D039 extends it to secretscan

guards.ref: internal/secretscan/secretscan_test.go — TestPatternsMatchKnownSecrets (positive + negative inputs for each of the 9 default patterns: aws/github-pat/github-fine/slack/jwt/google/pem/anthropic/openai) + TestScanFindsSecretInFile + TestScanRespectsIgnoreMarker + TestScanSkipsBinaryFilesByExtension + TestScanSkipsDefaultDirs + TestScanRespectsSuffixSkips + TestScanIgnoresOversizedFiles + TestScanWalksSubdirectoriesUnlessSkipped + TestRedactedExcerptHidesTheSecret. 10 tests total covering both pattern correctness and walker semantics

internal/secretscan/secretscan.go DefaultSkip extended with internal/secretscan/secretscan_test.go - matches the existing pattern for skipping secretscan.go itself (the test file legitimately contains pattern-matching strings for testing)

examples: TestPatternsMatchKnownSecrets covers each pattern with one positive and one+ negatives — e.g. AKIA + 16 uppercase chars matches, but AKIA + lowercase or AKIA + short doesn't.
TestScanFindsSecretInFile verifies the end-to-end Scan walks a temp directory, finds the secret in config.txt at line 2, and returns a Finding with the expected pattern + line + redacted excerpt.
TestScanRespectsIgnoreMarker verifies the `secretscan:ignore` comment on the same line as a fake secret suppresses the finding.
counterexamples: 5 other untested internal/ packages remain (conceptmap, devpattern, irdrift, jsonutil, knowledge) — D039 is scoped to secretscan as the security-critical priority; the others can be addressed in future decisions.

Tests verify the patterns CATCH known secrets, not that they don't match ALL possible non-secret strings — a regex with looser bounds would still pass tests. The walker semantics tests cover the structural skip rules.
